title: snapctl uses host locale
description:
[SRU] 2.75.2

[ Impact ]

Scripts are unable to depend upon output of formatted snapctl services queries due to it not respecting host locale.

[ Test Plan ]

1. Reproduce with snapd deb < 2.75

Install a language pack other than English and set it as default. In a snap shell, verify that snapctl services output is in the other language.

2. Prove fixed with snapd deb 2.75

Install a language pack other than English and set it as default. In a snap shell, verify that snapctl services output is only in English.

[ Where problems could occur ]

The change simply doesn't call translation for formated snapctl services queries. Potentially if some user were relying on that translated output in hooks then this change could require an update on their side. The risk of that seems negligible. 

---original---

Using snapctl from inside a snap follows the locale of the host system. The documentation at https://snapcraft.io/docs/using-snapctl implies that the responses are always in English, while in fact it is not. This makes it impossible to use snapctl from scripts in the snap, as all possible language variants would need to be handled.

This can be checked on a German system, ie. which has `language-pack-de` installed.

On the host the output of the `snap` command can be forced to English by setting the LANG environment variable to C or C.utf8:

```
$ snap services
Dienst                            Startvorgang  Aktuell  Notizen
gemma3-jane.server                aktiviert     inaktiv  -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service    aktiviert     aktiv    -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service-2  aktiviert     aktiv    -
$
$ LANG=C.utf8 snap services
Service                           Startup  Current   Notes
gemma3-jane.server                enabled  inactive  -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service    enabled  active    -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service-2  enabled  active    -
```

Inside a snap on this same system, using `snapctl`, it does not honor the LANG environment variable:
```
$ sudo snap run --shell go-snapctl-tester.test
#
# snapctl services
Dienst                            Startvorgang  Aktuell  Notizen
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service    aktiviert     aktiv    -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service-2  aktiviert     aktiv    -
#
# LANG=C.utf8 snapctl services
Dienst                            Startvorgang  Aktuell  Notizen
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service    aktiviert     aktiv    -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service-2  aktiviert     aktiv    -
```

messages.subject: snapctl uses host locale
messages.content:
Using snapctl from inside a snap follows the locale of the host system. The documentation at https://snapcraft.io/docs/using-snapctl implies that the responses are always in English, while in fact it is not. This makes it impossible to use snapctl from scripts in the snap, as all possible language variants would need to be handled.

This can be checked on a German system, ie. which has `language-pack-de` installed.

On the host the output of the `snap` command can be forced to English by setting the LANG environment variable to C or C.utf8:

```
$ snap services
Dienst                            Startvorgang  Aktuell  Notizen
gemma3-jane.server                aktiviert     inaktiv  -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service    aktiviert     aktiv    -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service-2  aktiviert     aktiv    -
$
$ LANG=C.utf8 snap services
Service                           Startup  Current   Notes
gemma3-jane.server                enabled  inactive  -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service    enabled  active    -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service-2  enabled  active    -
```

Inside a snap on this same system, using `snapctl`, it does not honor the LANG environment variable:
```
$ sudo snap run --shell go-snapctl-tester.test
#
# snapctl services
Dienst                            Startvorgang  Aktuell  Notizen
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service    aktiviert     aktiv    -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service-2  aktiviert     aktiv    -
#
# LANG=C.utf8 snapctl services
Dienst                            Startvorgang  Aktuell  Notizen
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service    aktiviert     aktiv    -
go-snapctl-tester.mock-service-2  aktiviert     aktiv    -
```
messages.author: jpm

messages.subject: Re: snapctl uses host locale
messages.content:
The issue seems to be the snapd REST API, which always responds using the host locale. Even the Accept-Language header is not honored:

# curl -sS --unix-socket /run/snapd-snap.socket \
     -X POST http://localhost/v2/snapctl \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -H "Accept-Language: en-US" \
     -d '{
           "context-id": "'"$SNAP_CONTEXT"'",
           "args": ["services"]
         }'
{"type":"sync","status-code":200,"status":"OK","result":{"stderr":"","stdout":"Dienst                            Startvorgang  Aktuell  Notizen\ngo-snapctl-tester.mock-service    aktiviert     aktiv    -\ngo-snapctl-tester.mock-service-2  aktiviert     aktiv    -\n"}}


The only alternative I can see is using the snapd-control interface, but this is super-privileged and won't be allowed for our snaps:

# curl -sS --unix-socket /run/snapd.socket "http://localhost/v2/apps?snap=$SNAP_NAME"
{"type":"sync","status-code":200,"status":"OK","result":[{"snap":"go-snapctl-tester","name":"mock-service","daemon":"simple","daemon-scope":"system","enabled":true,"active":true},{"snap":"go-snapctl-tester","name":"mock-service-2","daemon":"simple","daemon-scope":"system","enabled":true,"active":true},{"snap":"go-snapctl-tester","name":"test"},...]

Currently this bug is preventing our snaps from being installed on non-English systems (https://github.com/canonical/inference-snaps/issues/227). Are there any other known workarounds we could try?
messages.author: jpm

messages.subject: Re: snapctl uses host locale
messages.content:
The SnapD team and JP have discussed this a little more to better understand both the problem and any potential solutions.

The cause of the problem appears to be the snapd snapctl API response context running without a default locale, and snapd passing through what could be the default systemd service status response verbatim, according to the locale of the user's machine (German, in the case provided). 

Potential solutions discussed could be adopt LC_ALL=C.UTF-8 for the entire internal scope of snapd, or to ensure that the specific `service` API response (and other natural language responses) are always in English. These solutions need to be discussed a little more within the SnapD team to understand the implications.
messages.author: morrisong

messages.subject: Re: snapctl uses host locale
messages.content: This PR https://github.com/canonical/snapd/pull/16571 partially solves the problem by disabling translations for formatted output of "snapctl services" only.
messages.author: zeyadgouda

messages.subject: Re: snapctl uses host locale
messages.content: Adding a bug task for the snapd ubuntu package, and setting it to "fix committed" since 2.75.2 is in stonking-proposed already.
messages.author: ahasenack

messages.subject: Proposed package upload rejected
messages.content: An upload of snapd to resolute-proposed has been rejected from the upload queue for the following reason: "Please see comment https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/snapd/+bug/2143882/comments/1".
messages.author: ahasenack

messages.subject: Please test proposed package
messages.content:
Hello JP, or anyone else affected,

Accepted snapd into resolute-proposed. The package will build now and be available at https://launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/snapd/2.75.2+ubuntu26.04.2 in a few hours, and then in the -proposed repository.

Please help us by testing this new package.  See https://wiki.ubuntu.com/Testing/EnableProposed for documentation on how to enable and use -proposed.  Your feedback will aid us getting this update out to other Ubuntu users.

If this package fixes the bug for you, please add a comment to this bug, mentioning the version of the package you tested, what testing has been performed on the package and change the tag from verification-needed-resolute to verification-done-resolute. If it does not fix the bug for you, please add a comment stating that, and change the tag to verification-failed-resolute. In either case, without details of your testing we will not be able to proceed.

Further information regarding the verification process can be found at https://wiki.ubuntu.com/QATeam/PerformingSRUVerification .  Thank you in advance for helping!

N.B. The updated package will be released to -updates after the bug(s) fixed by this package have been verified and the package has been in -proposed for a minimum of 7 days.

messages.subject: Autopkgtest regression report (snapd/2.75.2+ubuntu26.04.2)
messages.content:
All autopkgtests for the newly accepted snapd (2.75.2+ubuntu26.04.2) for resolute have finished running.
The following regressions have been reported in tests triggered by the package:

samba/2:4.23.6+dfsg-1ubuntu2 (amd64)
snapd/2.75.2+ubuntu26.04.2 (amd64)


Please visit the excuses page listed below and investigate the failures, proceeding afterwards as per the StableReleaseUpdates policy regarding autopkgtest regressions [1].

https://ubuntu-archive-team.ubuntu.com/proposed-migration/resolute/update_excuses.html#snapd

[1] https://documentation.ubuntu.com/project/SRU/howto/autopkgtest-failure/

Thank you!

messages.content:
